edges.description: OCRL-dependent ciliary mechanosensation in trabecular meshwork cells is a plausible contributor to pressure dysregulation and glaucoma.

nodes.description: OCRL localizes to primary cilia; disease-associated OCRL loss shortens cilia and increases ciliary PI(4,5)P2, indicating a parallel ciliary phosphoinositide defect.

evidence.reference_title: OCRL localizes to the primary cilium: a new role for cilia in Lowe syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Lowe syndrome-associated mutations in OCRL result in shortened cilia and this phenotype can be rescued by the introduction of wild-type OCRL

evidence.reference_title: Loss of OCRL increases ciliary PI(4,5)P(2) in Lowe oculocerebrorenal syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Here, we demonstrate that cilia from Lowe syndrome patient fibroblasts exhibit increased levels of PI(4,5)P2 and decreased levels of PI4P.

nodes.description: OCRL loss causes ectopic PI(4,5)P2 accumulation on early endosomes, disturbing endosomal membrane composition.

evidence.reference_title: OCRL controls trafficking through early endosomes via PtdIns4,5P₂-dependent regulation of endosomal actin.
evidence.snippet: These trafficking defects are caused by ectopic accumulation of PtdIns4,5P(2) in EEs

nodes.description: Excess early-endosomal PI(4,5)P2 promotes abnormal F-actin accumulation and broader actin cytoskeletal instability.

evidence.reference_title: OCRL controls trafficking through early endosomes via PtdIns4,5P₂-dependent regulation of endosomal actin.
evidence.snippet: which in turn induces an N-WASP-dependent increase in endosomal F-actin.

evidence.reference_title: The deficiency of PIP2 5-phosphatase in Lowe syndrome affects actin polymerization.
evidence.snippet: we have identified a reproducible cellular abnormality of the actin cytoskeleton in fibroblasts from patients with Lowe syndrome.

nodes.description: Glaucoma is common, may be present concurrently with cataracts or develop after cataract surgery, and is a major cause of vision loss.

evidence.reference_title: Oculocerebrorenal syndrome of Lowe: Survey of ophthalmic presentations and management.
evidence.snippet: Glaucoma occurred in 54.7% of patients and affected both eyes in the majority of cases.

nodes.description: Low-molecular-weight proteinuria is the dominant protein-reabsorption defect of the renal phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Renal phenotype in Lowe Syndrome: a selective proximal tubular dysfunction.
evidence.snippet: All patients had low molecular weight proteinuria and albuminuria.

evidence.reference_title: Characterization of 28 novel patients expands the mutational and phenotypic spectrum of Lowe syndrome.
evidence.snippet: All patients carried OCRL gene defects with mutational hot spots at CpG dinucleotides.

evidence.reference_title: Identification of novel OCRL isoforms associated with phenotypic differences between Dent disease-2 and Lowe syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Truncating mutations in OCRL exons 1-7 lead to Dent disease-2, whereas those in exons 8-24 lead to Lowe syndrome.
OCRL | HGNC:8108 | oculocerebrorenal syndrome | MONDO:0010645 | XL | Definitive

nodes.description: Pathogenic OCRL variants reduce the 5-phosphatase activity that normally dephosphorylates phosphatidylinositol 4,5-bisphosphate, shifting phosphoinositide homeostasis on internal membranes.

evidence.reference_title: The protein deficient in Lowe syndrome is a phosphatidylinositol-4,5-bisphosphate 5-phosphatase.
evidence.snippet: These results suggest that OCRL is mainly a lipid phosphatase that may control cellular levels of a critical metabolite, phosphatidylinositol 4,5-bisphosphate.

nodes.description: In proximal tubule epithelial cells, defective OCRL-dependent trafficking reduces megalin recycling and impairs receptor-mediated uptake of filtered low-molecular-weight proteins.

evidence.reference_title: OCRL controls trafficking through early endosomes via PtdIns4,5P₂-dependent regulation of endosomal actin.
evidence.snippet: OCRL depletion impairs the recycling of multiple classes of receptors, including megalin (which mediates protein reabsorption in the kidney) that are retained in engorged EEs.

evidence.reference_title: The Lowe syndrome protein OCRL1 is required for endocytosis in the zebrafish pronephric tubule.
evidence.snippet: Here, we show that deficiency in OCRL1 causes a defect in endocytosis in the zebrafish pronephric tubule, a model for the mammalian renal tubule.

nodes.description: Abnormal phosphoinositide-actin coupling disrupts clathrin-mediated trafficking and recycling of cargo receptors through endosomes.

evidence.reference_title: Lowe syndrome protein OCRL1 interacts with clathrin and regulates protein trafficking between endosomes and the trans-Golgi network.
evidence.snippet: These findings suggest a role for OCRL1 in clathrin-mediated trafficking of proteins from endosomes to the TGN and that defects in this pathway might contribute to the Lowe syndrome phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: A role of OCRL in clathrin-coated pit dynamics and uncoating revealed by studies of Lowe syndrome cells.
evidence.snippet: Here we show that OCRL loss in Lowe syndrome patient fibroblasts impacts clathrin-mediated endocytosis and results in an endocytic defect.

nodes.description: The renal phenotype is a selective proximal tubulopathy rather than a fully expressed generalized Fanconi syndrome in all patients.

evidence.reference_title: Renal phenotype in Lowe Syndrome: a selective proximal tubular dysfunction.
evidence.snippet: Patients with Lowe syndrome do not have renal Fanconi syndrome but a selective proximal tubulopathy, variable in extent and dominated by low molecular weight proteinuria and hypercalciuria, the classical features of Dent disease.
